title: Tasmanian government breaks election promise over TasInsure as 'insurance company' overhauled
full_text:
The Rockliff government will break an election promise to offer insurance to all Tasmanians through a state-owned insurance agency, instead establishing a new body to "oversee and support the insurance ecosystem".
TasInsure was the flagship policy of the Liberals in last year's snap state election campaign, with Premier Jeremy Rockliff saying the insurance market had "failed Tasmanians".
"TasInsure will offer affordable insurance for home, contents, small business, community groups and events, and regional insurance," he said when announcing it last July.
They repeatedly promised it would save families $250 a year and small businesses 20 per cent on their insurance costs.
The state government now says TasInsure will be a "not-for-profit statutory authority" with a "primary focus" of lowering the cost of insurance.
"TasInsure will partner with insurers, brokers, reinsurers and other parts of the insurance system to address gaps in availability and affordability," the government said in a statement.
The government has released an implementation plan for TasInsure, which will be established with legislation.
"TasInsure will step in where the market fails and will focus on long-term affordability by addressing risk, prevention, resilience and increasing competition," Mr Rockliff said.
The government says its now "finalising TasInsure's initial market interventions to support hard-to-insure activities and risks", and will also "develop advisory, transparency and comparison functions to promote competition".
Asked if this was a broken promise, Mr Rockliff said he believed the government could "better our commitments" through this path.
RACT chief executive Mark Mugnaioni welcomed the state government's implementation plan, calling it "a positive step towards improving insurance affordability".
TasInsure would have been a major competitor to RACT, Tasmania's major state-based insurance agency.
Both the RACT and the national body representing insurers have criticised the TasInsure policy, calling it the wrong solution to rising insurance costs.
Analysis by LateralEconomics, ordered by the industry, concluded it would be "costly and risky for the state government", and would lose up to $13 million annually.
summary: The Tasmanian government has changed its plan for TasInsure, a state-owned insurance agency, now focusing on overseeing the insurance market instead of directly providing insurance. Premier Jeremy Rockliff stated this new approach aims to improve affordability and competition in the insurance sector.
author: state political Josh Dugga
heading: General Insurance
key_points: Rockliff government breaks promise to offer direct insurance through TasInsure.
TasInsure will now be a not-for-profit authority to support the insurance market.
Initial plans include addressing gaps in insurance availability and affordability.
RACT CEO Mark Mugnaioni supports the new implementation plan.
Critics say the original TasInsure plan was costly and risky.
ticker_line: Tasmanian government alters TasInsure insurance plan
